tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: All four f2p tests are purely text-matching: they open .rb files and assert specific strings exist in content (e.g., 'SecureRandom.hex(12) in content', 'local_podspecs_dir in content'). No test imports, calls, or executes the Ruby code to verify behavior. The p2p tests do run 'ruby -c' for syntax checks, but f2p tests are entirely string-in-file checks.

test_not_tautological.reason: An agent could simply append a comment or dead code containing the magic strings (e.g., '# SecureRandom.hex(12)', '# local_podspecs_dir', '# xcfw_info', '# rm_rf(pod_dir)', '# define_singleton_method(:generate_available_uuid_list)') to pass all f2p tests without implementing any actual fix. The tests only check string presence, not functional behavior.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution is at /solution/solve.sh and /solution/gold.patch in the container. The agent could read these files directly (cat /solution/gold.patch) and apply the exact patch. There are no measures preventing access to the /solution/ directory.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors described in the instruction (UUID singleton method, SecureRandom usage, local podspecs clearing, stale pod dir removal) are tested. The tests cover both the UUID collision fix and the stale cache fix.
